Patch Set 11:

fixed lnet-selftest failure.

Patch Set 11:

I'm seeing the following build error:

/data/buildsystem/jsimmons-head/rpmbuild/usr/src/lustre-2.6.92/lnet/lnet/config.c:125: error: static declaration of ‘lnet_ni_alloc’ follows non-static declaration
/data/buildsystem/jsimmons-head/rpmbuild/usr/src/lustre-2.6.92/lnet/include/lnet/lib-lnet.h:715: note: previous declaration of ‘lnet_ni_alloc’ was here

The lnet_ni_alloc function is used in both config.c and api-ni.c. I don't think it can be static.

Patch Set 11:

As a note I'm testing this with the LU-5734.
Uploaded patch set 12.

Patch Set 12:

LU-5734 is creating that error. Assuming LU-5734 will go upstream, I've let lnet_ni_alloc be global as before.

Patch Set 13:

Frank, have you checked that none of these symbols are exported? Unfortunately exporting a static symbol does not seem to be detected by a normal build. But it causes a weird section mismatch error in the PPC build.

Patch Set 13:

What errors are you seeing?

Patch Set 13:

If that's the case the module wouldn't load. There was a case like that earlier, in a module that wasn't normally loaded (lnet-selftest) and was detected by the build system. Otherwise I've checked by using llmount.sh to ensure the regular modules are loading properly.

Patch Set 13:

Thanks John. I'll fix these.

Patch Set 13: -Code-Review

Also LU-5374 also landed so this needs a rebase anyways.

parents.subject: Revert "LU-5570 lnet: check router aliveness timestamp"

commit.subject: LU-5396 lnet: make some functions static
commit.message:
LU-5396 lnet: make some functions static

Some functions and variables are only used in their C file, so reduce
their scope. This reduces the code size, and fixes sparse warnings
such as:

  warning: symbol 'proc_lnet_routes' was not declared.
      Should it be static?
  warning: symbol 'proc_lnet_routers' was not declared.
      Should it be static?

Some prototypes were removed from C files and added to the proper
header.

Change-Id: I6b0c527d64ea9e3394cc686699d2e75eec5c16a2
Signed-off-by: Frank Zago <fzago@cray.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/12206
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
